string.text: Master switch for the addon. When disabled, ZHOPA stops assigning tasks and running its economy, loot, artefact, and story systems.
Allows ZHOPA to control stalker squad movement and assign stalker tasks such as rest, patrol, hunt, artefact search, and trade.
Allows ZHOPA to control mutant squad movement and assign mutant tasks such as roaming, hunting, patrol, and rest.
Shows ZHOPA squad state on map markers and enables extra diagnostic console messages for debugging simulation behavior.
Automatically fills marked smart terrains with missing trader, technician, medic, barman, or guide service squads when the smart needs them.
Lets online NPCs use ZHOPA looting rules for corpses and loose items, including targeted item pickup and stuck-loot recovery.
Allows squads that fight or loot outside the loaded level to gain loot value and loot cargo in the offline simulation.
Move real items during offline looting
When possible, transfers actual server-side items from the victim squad to the looter squad instead of only adding abstract loot value.
Allows offline artefact tasks to use virtual artefacts generated from anomaly settings when no suitable real artefact exists. Real artefacts are still preferred.
Enables squad trade behavior: online sales to traders, offline barter accounting, automatic rest/base trading, and dedicated trade route tasks.
Sets what fraction of item value NPC squads receive when selling gear to traders. A lower value slows squad income without changing NPC purchases.
Removes known SISKI 1.0 and ZHOPA 1.01 save blocks and stale runtime markers during load/save so old saves can migrate to ZHOPA ALIFE 2.
Only reports detected legacy save data in debug mode without deleting it. Use this to inspect a save before applying cleanup.
Also clears current ZHOPA2 task and story state before saving. Enable only when preparing a save for removing this addon.
In story mode, converts non-immune stalker squads on active psi levels into zombified squads. Immune factions are controlled by config.
After the Brain Scorcher is disabled in story mode, sends selected eligible stalker squads toward northern levels instead of leaving them in place.
Percent of eligible stalker squads selected for the northern migration event. Higher values move more squads toward Zaton, Jupiter, and Pripyat.
Allows stalker squads to choose exploration targets and travel to valid smart terrains that are useful for roaming.
Allows stalker squads to move into suitable smart terrains that need occupants or local presence.
Allows stalker squads to settle into base camp slots and provide persistent presence at valid base smart terrains.
Allows stalker squads to patrol between several valid smart terrains instead of staying at one location.
Allows stalker squads to pause movement during the night and then resume their previous task when rest is finished.
Allows stalker squads to actively pursue hostile squads or direct hunt targets instead of only moving between smart terrains.
Allows stalker squads to travel to anomaly smart terrains and collect artefacts through the online or offline artefact logic.
Relative chance for a stalker squad to pick exploration when several task types are available. Set to 0 to remove it from weighted selection.
Relative chance for a stalker squad to choose a smart terrain that needs population or local presence.
Relative chance for a stalker squad to pick a patrol route between smart terrains.
Relative chance for a stalker squad to hunt enemies when a valid target exists.
Relative chance for a stalker squad to choose an artefact task when a valid artefact target exists.
Base chance for a stalker squad to choose a dedicated trade route. The chance increases when the squad carries valuable items to sell.
How many in-game hours a stalker squad spends in regular rest before it can select another task.
How many in-game hours a stalker squad keeps patrolling before it can roll a new task.
Minimum real-time seconds a stalker squad waits in fallback rest when no valid task can be assigned.
Maximum real-time seconds a stalker squad waits in fallback rest when no valid task can be assigned.
Keeps the squad's current, assigned, and scripted target fields aligned so smart terrain logic, debug HUD, and task logic agree on the destination.
Stops online smart terrain jobs from pulling NPCs away from the squad's active ZHOPA task when vanilla logic tries to reassign them.
Allows squads to remember recent attackers and create revenge tasks against the squad that attacked them.
Allows hostile squads to target the actor with revenge behavior when the actor caused the retaliation event.
Percent chance that a valid revenge event caused by the actor becomes an active revenge task.
Allows hunt tasks to resolve damage, loot, and outcome while both squads are offline and close enough to fight.
Real-time seconds between offline combat checks for active hunt tasks.
Distance in meters at which two offline squads are considered close enough for hunt combat to resolve.
Allows mutant squads to roam toward valid smart terrains when they are not hunting or patrolling.
Allows mutant squads to search for and pursue hostile stalker squads according to faction relations and available targets.
Allows mutant squads to patrol between valid smart terrains instead of remaining in rest.
Relative chance for a mutant squad to choose a hunt task when a valid enemy target exists.
Relative chance for a mutant squad to choose a patrol route between smart terrains.
Relative chance for a mutant squad to choose free roaming when it has no stronger reason to hunt or patrol.
How many in-game hours a mutant squad spends resting before it can select another task.
How many in-game hours a mutant squad keeps patrolling before it can roll a new task.
